Dear Ms. Abt:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
San Jose Airport Commissionei Robert Thompson with respect to the
commissioner;s respons ibi I ities under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the political 
Reform Act (the rrg6grr1'l
OUEST IONS
1. Does San Jose AirPort Corn:nissioner Robert Thonpson have a
conflict of interest with respect to AirPort Commission decisions
concerning the Public Affairs Council?
2. Does San Jose Airport conmissioner Robert Thompson have
an economic i.nterest in the nenbers of the board of directors of
the PubIic Affairs council or in American Airlines?
CONCLUS I ONS
1. Airport commissioner Robert Thompson is.prohibited. from
maxinq, participating in :naking, or otherwise using his official
position-to influence a governmental decision that will
ioreseeably and materialiy affect the public Affairs CounciI, or
which wouli achieve, defeit, aide, or hinder a goal or purpose of
the PubIic Affairs counc i I .
I Go.r"ar,r"nt Code Sections 81OOO-91O15. AII statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated' 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 cal-ifornia code of Regulations . Section
18600, et seo.- AIl references to regulations are to Title 2,
oivj,sion 6 of the californj'a code of Regulations'
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322-5660


2. It does not appear that the conmissioner has an economic
interest in the directois of the PubIic Affairs Council, and
therefore, the commissioner is not restricted v/ith respect to
participating in decisions concerning the interests of the
inalivialual directors. 
l,toreover, Anerican Airlines rrill not be an
economic interest of the commissioner so long as the free tickets
are provided to the Public Affairs council in lieu of menbership
dues for general council use and not for the use of a particular
councilmember.
FACTS
cornmissioner Robert Thompson is an appointed commissioner on
the San Jose Airport Cornmission (the [conmission") and serves as
the commission,s chairman. In addition, the comrnissioner serves
in li.s private capacity as president of the PubIic Affairs council
(the "ctunciI"), a nonprofit corporation established as a tax-
exenpt organization under Section 501(c) (3) of the Internal
Reveirue C5de, and Section Z37O1d of the Revenue and Taxation Code.
The council was established to promote a strong and healthy
economy in santa clara and san Benito counties consistent with
enviroinental concerns in the area. The council is controlled by
a thirty-rnenber board of directors which includes corporate
members'. The board deterrnines the nembership of the organization
and sets the compensation to be paid to Commissioner Thompson as
president. MenbLrship dues are used. for operational expenses,
i."f"Jl"g 
salaries. ihe council actively Iobbies the Ieqislature
in iurtfr6rance of its policy goals. As president of the council,
conmissioner Thonpson is active in recruiting nee, nembers for the
organization.
Anerican AirIines, Inc. is currentLy seeking to join the
council as a voting rnenber of the board of directors. 
The airline
has proposed providing free transportation for the.president and
othei council officials 
in Iieu of paying membership dues'z
American Airlines, Inc. is also a najor tenant at the San Jose
International Airport and issues affecting the operations of alI
airlines, including American Airlines, Inc., regularlY come before
the Airport Cornmission for review and recommendat ion. r
Additionally, another nenber of the council's board of
directors is also an investor in one of San Jose Airport's
tenants, the san Jose Jet center, Inc. Matters affecting the
tenants of the airport, including the san Jose Jet center, Inc.,

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Cornmission August 20, L99L Evet Abt Chief Deputy c j.ty Attorney city of San Jose 151 West Mission Street San Jose, CA 9 511O ＼ Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-91-3 61 Dear Ms. Abt: This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of San Jose Airport Commissionei Robert Thompson with respect to the commissioner;s respons ibi I ities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the political Reform Act (the rrg6grr1'l OUEST IONS 1. Does San Jose AirPort Corn:nissioner Robert Thonpson have a conflict of interest with respect to AirPort Commission decisions concerning the Public Affairs Council? 2. Does San Jose Airport conmissioner Robert Thompson have an economic i.nterest in the nenbers of the board of directors of the PubIic Affairs council or in American Airlines? CONCLUS I ONS 1. Airport commissioner Robert Thompson is.prohibited. from maxinq, participating in :naking, or otherwise using his official position-to influence a governmental decision that will ioreseeably and materialiy affect the public Affairs CounciI, or which wouli achieve, defeit, aide, or hinder a goal or purpose of the PubIic Affairs counc i I . I Go.r"ar,r"nt Code Sections 81OOO-91O15. AII statutory references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated' Commission regulations appear at 2 cal-ifornia code of Regulations . Section 18600, et seo.- AIl references to regulations are to Title 2, oivj,sion 6 of the californj'a code of Regulations' 428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322-5660 2. It does not appear that the conmissioner has an economic interest in the directois of the PubIic Affairs Council, and therefore, the commissioner is not restricted v/ith respect to participating in decisions concerning the interests of the inalivialual directors. l,toreover, Anerican Airlines rrill not be an economic interest of the commissioner so long as the free tickets are provided to the Public Affairs council in lieu of menbership dues for general council use and not for the use of a particular councilmember. FACTS cornmissioner Robert Thompson is an appointed commissioner on the San Jose Airport Cornmission (the [conmission") and serves as the commission,s chairman. In addition, the comrnissioner serves in li.s private capacity as president of the PubIic Affairs council (the "ctunciI"), a nonprofit corporation established as a tax- exenpt organization under Section 501(c) (3) of the Internal Reveirue C5de, and Section Z37O1d of the Revenue and Taxation Code. The council was established to promote a strong and healthy economy in santa clara and san Benito counties consistent with enviroinental concerns in the area. The council is controlled by a thirty-rnenber board of directors which includes corporate members'. The board deterrnines the nembership of the organization and sets the compensation to be paid to Commissioner Thompson as president. MenbLrship dues are used. for operational expenses, i."f"Jl"g salaries. ihe council actively Iobbies the Ieqislature in iurtfr6rance of its policy goals. As president of the council, conmissioner Thonpson is active in recruiting nee, nembers for the organization. Anerican AirIines, Inc.
